title: 10 best herbal tinctures for overactive bladder
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for overactive bladder are concentrated liquid extracts made from plants that have been traditionally used to support urinary health. These natural remedies help alleviate symptoms by reducing muscle spasms and relaxing the bladder, allowing for more efficient urination. Examples of herbal tinctures that show promise in managing overactive bladder include St. John's Wort, which helps regulate nerve function, and Marshmallow root, which soothes and calms the urinary tract. By taking these tinctures, individuals can experience a reduction in frequency and urgency of urination, improving their quality of life and reducing anxiety related to accidents or leakage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bearberry tinctures helps with overactive bladder because it contains anthraquinones, a natural compound that has been shown to have a calming effect on the muscles in the urinary tract. This relaxation of the muscles can help reduce the frequency and urgency of urination, as well as alleviate symptoms such as leakage and discomfort. Additionally, bearberry tinctures may also have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe any irritation or inflammation in the bladder and urethra, further reducing symptoms associated with overactive bladder.
remedy_properties: Urosorbic acid: This property helps to reduce the frequency of urination by absorbing excess urine in the bladder and preventing it from overflowing, alleviating symptoms of overactive bladder.
Tannins: Tannins have astringent properties that help to contract the muscles in the urinary tract, reducing spasms and urgency, which can contribute to frequent urination and incontinence.

Anthraquinones: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which may help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the urinary tract, leading to improved bladder control and reduced symptoms of overactive bladder.
remedy_constituents: Ursolic acid: This triterpenoid compound helps with overactive bladder by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the bladder, which can contribute to symptoms of overactive bladder.
Arbutin: This phenolic glycoside has been shown to relax the smooth muscle in the bladder and reduce the frequency of urination, making it a potential natural treatment for overactive bladder.

Tannins: These polyphenolic compounds have astringent properties that may help to reduce the frequency of urination and alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder by constricting the bladder and reducing its irritability.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used because they are known to contain arbutin, a compound that helps relax the bladder muscles and reduce symptoms of overactive bladder.
Roots: The roots are used because they are rich in arbutin and other bioactive compounds that help relax the bladder muscles, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of clean dried arctostaphylos uva ursi leaves and stems in the summer months.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces to increase the surface area.
Combine the chopped plant material with 2 parts of 95% ethanol in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks with occasional shaking.
Filter the liquid through a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Saw palmetto tinctures helps with overactive bladder because it has a natural ability to relax the muscles in the urinary tract, reducing symptoms of urgency and frequency. The herb's primary compound, beta-sitosterol, blocks the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), which is known to cause an increase in prostatic size and contribute to overactive bladder syndrome. By balancing hormonal levels and relaxing the muscles, saw palmetto tinctures can help alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder, providing relief for those affected by this common condition.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Saw palmetto's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the urinary tract, which can contribute to symptoms of overactive bladder such as frequency and urgency.

Alpha-reductase inhibition: Saw palmetto has been shown to inhibit the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that can exacerbate bladder spasms and symptoms of overactive bladder.

Muscle relaxing properties: The fatty acid content in saw palmetto, particularly beta-sitosterol, has been found to relax smooth muscle tissue, which can help reduce bladder contractions and alleviate symptoms of urgency and frequency.
remedy_constituents: Iridoids: Iridoids, such as serenoa glycoside, may help alleviate overactive bladder symptoms by reducing inflammation and modulating the activity of certain neurotransmitters involved in bladder control.

Phenolic acids (Ferulic acid and Gallic acid): Phenolic acids in saw palmetto may help decrease inflammation and oxidative stress in the bladder, which can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms, thereby reducing discomfort and improving bladder control.

Lignans (Acuminatin and Isomers): Lignans in saw palmetto may help relax the smooth muscle in the bladder and urethra, reducing muscle spasms and improving bladder capacity, which can lead to relief from overactive bladder symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high concentration of saw palmetto extract, which has been traditionally used to support urinary health.
Leaves: They are used as an alternative to roots, as they also contain saw palmetto extract and have similar properties.
Rhyzomes: They are used as they contain a high concentration of saw palmetto extract, similar to roots, and are used in some herbal remedies for urinary health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of serenoa repens roots and rhizomes in the fall or spring for optimal potency.
Chop 1 part of the harvested plant material into smaller pieces to increase surface area.
Mix 1 part of chopped plant material with 2-3 parts of 80% ethanol or glycerin in a glass container.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks or up to 8 weeks for stronger tincture.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle after the steeping period.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tinctures helps with overactive bladder because of its soothing properties that calm the urinary tract and reduce inflammation. The herb's mucilage, a thick, protective substance, coats and shields the bladder lining, relieving irritation and discomfort. Additionally, marshmallow tincture has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which help to repair and regenerate damaged tissue, reducing frequency and urgency of urination. This natural remedy can provide long-lasting relief for individuals suffering from overactive bladder symptoms.
remedy_properties: Demulcent and soothing properties: Marshmallow root helps to soothe and calm the mucous membranes in the urinary tract, reducing irritation and inflammation associated with overactive bladder.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The saponins present in marshmallow root have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the bladder and urethra, alleviating symptoms of overactive bladder.

Astringent and toning effects: Marshmallow root has astringent and toning properties that help to strengthen and tone the muscles in the urinary tract, improving bladder control and reducing symptoms of urgency and frequency.
remedy_constituents: Polysaccharides: These complex carbohydrates help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the bladder, reducing irritation and inflammation that can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms.

Phenolic glycosides (such as althaea saponins): These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce bladder muscle spasms and improve bladder function in individuals with overactive bladder.

Flavonoids (such as kaempferol and quercetin): These plant-derived compounds have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to reduce bladder irritation and inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Althaea officinalis are used due to their high mucilage content, which helps to soothe and calm the bladder muscles.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their ability to relax the bladder muscles and reduce inflammation, providing relief from overactive bladder symptoms.
Barks: The barks are used for their medicinal properties, including their ability to calm the bladder and reduce spasms, which helps to manage overactive bladder symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest approximately 100 grams of fresh or dried roots of althaea officinalis.
Chop the harvested roots into small pieces to increase their surface area for infusion.
Combine the chopped roots with 500 milliliters of high-proof ethanol in a clean glass container.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for a minimum of two weeks to allow infusion.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Corn tinctures helps with overactive bladder because they contain a unique combination of herbs that work together to soothe and calm the urinary tract. The active ingredients, such as corn silk and uva ursi, help to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the bladder and urethra, allowing for easier urination and fewer frequent trips to the bathroom. Additionally, these herbs may also help to strengthen the pelvic floor muscles, further reducing the symptoms of overactive bladder.
remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Astringent herbs like corn silk and marshmallow root help reduce urine production by constricting blood vessels in the urinary tract, which can alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder such as frequent urination.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal tinctures containing anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids (found in corn silk, uva ursi, and plantain) help reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, which can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms and discomfort.

Antispasmodic properties: Antispasmodic herbs like passionflower and wild yam relax muscles in the pelvic floor and urinary tract, reducing spasms that can lead to frequent urination, incontinence, and other overactive bladder symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids in Zea mays tinctures may help alleviate overactive bladder symptoms by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the urinary tract.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Zea mays may contribute to its potential benefits for overactive bladder by relaxing the smooth muscle in the urinary tract, reducing spasms and improving bladder control.
Ferulic acid: Ferulic acid, a phenolic acid in Zea mays, may help mitigate overactive bladder symptoms by inhibiting the contraction of bladder smooth muscle and reducing the frequency of urination.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Rich in nutrients and bioactive compounds that may help alleviate bladder symptoms.
Leaves: Contain various bioactive compounds that may help relax the bladder muscles and reduce symptoms of overactive bladder.
Stems: May be used to create a tincture that helps to soothe and calm the bladder, reducing symptoms of overactive bladder.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part of zea mays flowers and leaves and 2 parts of high-proof vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar.
Combine the zea mays flowers and leaves with the vodka or glycerin in the jar and seal it tightly.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks shaking it daily to infuse the zea mays.
Strain the zea mays tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar discard the solids.
Transfer the zea mays tincture to dark glass bottles and store them in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal tinctures helps with overactive bladder because it contains berberine, a natural compound that has been shown to relax the muscles of the urinary tract, reducing spasms and frequency. Berberine also has antimicrobial properties, which can help combat bacterial infections that may contribute to bladder irritability. Additionally, goldenseal's anti-inflammatory effects can soothe irritated bladder tissue, reducing discomfort and urgency, providing relief from frequent urination and incontinence episodes.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Berberine has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help soothe and calm the muscles in the bladder and urethra, reducing symptoms of urgency and frequency.

Hydroxyberberine: Hydroxyberberine is a bioactive compound that exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the urinary tract, alleviating symptoms of overactive bladder.
Canadine: Canadine has been found to have a calming effect on smooth muscle contractions, including those in the bladder and urethra, potentially reducing the frequency and urgency of urination.
remedy_constituents: Berberine: Berberine, an alkaloid compound found in Hydrastis canadensis, may help alleviate overactive bladder symptoms by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the bladder, thereby promoting relaxation and reducing muscle spasms.

Hydrastine: Hydrastine, a benzylisoquinoline alkaloid in Hydrastis canadensis, has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may contribute to its potential benefits in reducing urinary tract infections and inflammation associated with overactive bladder.

Hydrastis glycoside (Berbigenin): Hydrastis glycoside, a phenolic glycoside in Hydrastis canadensis, may help relax the bladder muscle and reduce muscle spasms, thereby alleviating symptoms of overactive bladder.
remedy_parts: Roots: The root of Hydrastis canadensis is considered the most effective part due to its high concentration of berberine, a compound that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes of Hydrastis canadensis are also used as they are believed to contain similar compounds to the roots, although to a lesser extent.
Leaves: Leaves of Hydrastis canadensis contain smaller amounts of berberine but are still used in some cases due to their potential to provide relief from overactive bladder symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of fresh hydrastis canadensis roots in the fall season.
Clean the roots thoroughly with water to remove dirt and debris.
Dry the roots in a single layer at room temperature for 2-3 weeks.
Chop 1 part of dried roots into smaller pieces and combine with 2 parts of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks, shaking the jar daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort tinctures helps with overactive bladder because of its ability to calm the nervous system and reduce inflammation in the urinary tract. The herb has a long history of use as a natural relaxant, which can help alleviate symptoms such as urgency, frequency, and nocturia. Additionally, St John's Wort may also help to reduce muscle spasms in the bladder and urethra, allowing for more efficient emptying of the bladder and reducing the risk of leakage or accidents.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: Helps to relax the smooth muscle in the bladder wall, reducing contractions and frequency of urination. This reduces symptoms of overactive bladder such as urgency and frequency.
Flavonoids and phenolic acids: Possess anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the urinary tract that may contribute to overactive bladder symptoms.
Hyporin: Exhibits antispasmodic effects, helping to relax the muscles in the bladder neck and urethra, reducing spasms and discomfort associated with overactive bladder.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: A phenolic compound that has been shown to relax smooth muscle in the bladder, reducing spasms and alleviating symptoms of overactive bladder.

Naphthodianthrone derivatives: Compounds that have been found to inhibit the growth of bacteria that can contribute to urinary tract infections (UTIs), which can exacerbate overactive bladder symptoms.

Flavonoids (including rutin and hyperoside): Plant compounds that have antioxidant properties, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the bladder, potentially alleviating overactive bladder symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain flavonoids and xanthones with potential anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help alleviate bladder spasms.
Flowers: They have been traditionally used to treat urinary issues due to their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help reduce bladder irritation and spasms.
Roots: They are rich in hypericin and hyperforin, which have potential anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that can help soothe the urinary tract and reduce bladder spasms.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part of dried hypericum perforatum flowers in a clean glass jar to ensure quality.
Combine the dried flowers with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in the glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks to allow infusion to occur.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into another clean glass container to remove solids.
Transfer the resulting tincture to a dark glass bottle and store it in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with overactive bladder because of its natural ability to calm the urinary tract and reduce inflammation. The herbal extract has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the bladder lining, reducing irritation and frequency of urination. Additionally, dandelion tincture can help relax the muscles in the pelvic floor, which can become tense and contribute to overactive bladder symptoms such as urgency and incontinence. By promoting a healthy balance in the urinary tract, dandelion tinctures provide relief from frequent trips to the bathroom and associated discomfort.
remedy_properties: Diuretic Properties: Dandelion tincture helps to increase urine production, which can alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder such as frequent urination and urinary retention by flushing out toxins and excess fluids from the body.

Anti-Inflammatory and Antispasmodic Properties: The compounds present in dandelion tincture have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which can help to reduce inflammation in the bladder and urethra, relax muscle spasms that can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms, and alleviate discomfort and pain associated with urinary frequency.

Antioxidant Properties: Dandelion tincture is rich in antioxidants, which can help to protect the bladder lining from damage caused by oxidative stress and inflammation, reducing the risk of chronic conditions like interstitial cystitis and improving overall bladder health.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: These triterpenoid compounds have been shown to have a relaxing effect on the bladder muscles, reducing the urge to urinate frequently.
Taraxasterol: This triterpenoid has anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate bladder inflammation and irritation associated with overactive bladder.
Flavonoids: These phenolic compounds, including quercetin and kaempferol, have been found to have a relaxing effect on the bladder and may help reduce the frequency of urination.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their high concentration of saponins, which have been shown to have a relaxing effect on the bladder muscles.
Leaves: The leaves are used due to their high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which may help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder.
Flowers: The flowers are used due to their ability to stimulate the bladder's parasympathetic nervous system, promoting relaxation and reducing muscle spasms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion roots and leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated when temperatures are mild.
Clean and dry the harvested dandelion roots and leaves thoroughly using paper towels for 2 hours.
Chop 500 grams of dried dandelion roots and leaves into fine particles using a coffee grinder for 10 minutes.
Combine the chopped dandelion particles with 1000 milliliters of vodka in a clean glass jar for 6 weeks.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop tinctures helps with overactive bladder because of its unique properties that soothe and calm the urinary tract. The active compounds in maypop, such as flavonoids and terpenes, have a natural ability to reduce muscle spasms and relax the bladder muscles, allowing for more efficient urine storage and reduced frequency of urination. Additionally, maypop's anti-inflammatory properties help to alleviate irritation and discomfort associated with overactive bladder, promoting a healthier urinary tract overall.
remedy_properties: Flavonoids: Maypop contains flavonoids, which have been shown to relax the muscles in the bladder and pelvic floor, reducing symptoms of urinary urgency and frequency.
Tannins: The high concentration of tannins in Maypop may help to reduce inflammation in the bladder and urethra, alleviating discomfort and pain associated with overactive bladder.

Amino acids: Maypop contains a range of amino acids, including flavonoid-derived ones, which can help to increase blood flow to the bladder and pelvic floor, promoting healing and reducing symptoms of overactive bladder.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant-based compounds have been shown to have a relaxing effect on the smooth muscle in the bladder, which can help alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder.

Alkaloids (specifically, Harman and Harmin): These compounds have been found to have anxiolytic and sedative properties, which can help reduce stress and anxiety that may contribute to overactive bladder symptoms.

Iridoids (specifically, Inuline): These compounds have been found to have a relaxing effect on the smooth muscle in the bladder and may also have a mild diuretic effect, which can help alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids and alkaloids which have a relaxing effect on the bladder and urinary tract.
Roots: The roots of Passiflora incarnata contain a high concentration of passifloric acid, which is believed to help reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the bladder.
Fruits: The pulp and juice of the fruit contain flavonoids and other compounds that may help to reduce the frequency of urination and alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1:8 to 1:10 ratio of dried plant material to solvent at peak flower and fruit stage.
Dry the freshly harvested plant material at 30-40°c for 24-48 hours to remove moisture.
Combine 250ml of solvent such as 80-95% ethanol or glycerin with 25 grams of dried plant material.
Steep the mixture in a clean glass container for 2-6 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tinctures helps with overactive bladder because it has a calming effect on the urinary tract, reducing spasms and contractions that can cause frequent urination. The herb's active compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and citral, work together to soothe the muscles in the bladder and urethra, allowing for more relaxed and efficient urination. By promoting relaxation and reducing tension, lemon balm tinctures help alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder, providing relief from frequent trips to the bathroom.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Lemon balm's antispasmodic effects can help relax the muscles in the bladder and urinary tract, reducing spasms and cramps that contribute to overactive bladder symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lemon balm may reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, which can help alleviate discomfort and frequency associated with overactive bladder.

Uterotonics and anticholinergic effects: Lemon balm's uterotonic effects can help strengthen the bladder muscles, while its anticholinergic properties can relax the detrusor muscle, reducing contractions that cause frequent urination.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce muscle spasms and inflammation associated with overactive bladder.
Linalool: This terpene has a calming effect on the nervous system, which may help reduce the frequency and urgency of urination by regulating bladder contractions.
Luteolin: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which may help relax the bladder muscles and reduce the symptoms of overactive bladder, such as urgency and frequency.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Melissa officinalis are used to make tinctures for overactive bladder because of their high content of volatile oils, particularly linalool and linalyl acetate, which have been shown to relax the muscles and reduce bladder spasms.

Roots: The roots of Melissa officinalis are used to make tinctures for overactive bladder because they contain a higher concentration of alkaloids and saponins, which have been found to have antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties.

Stems: The stems of Melissa officinalis are used to make tinctures for overactive bladder because they contain a mixture of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have a relaxing effect on the smooth muscles of the bladder and reduce symptoms of overactive bladder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves and flowers in the morning when they are at their peak potency.
Dry the plant material in a single layer at a temperature below 105 degrees fahrenheit for 1 to 2 hours.
Combine 1 part of the dried plant material with 2 parts of a neutral solvent like vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 to 6 weeks in a cool dark place, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth and discard the solids, transfer the tincture to a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover tinctures helps with overactive bladder because it contains isoflavones, which have been shown to relax the muscles in the bladder and urethra, reducing frequency and urgency of urination. Additionally, red clover's anti-inflammatory properties may help soothe the bladder lining and reduce inflammation that can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms. By calming the muscle contractions and reducing inflammation, red clover tinctures may provide natural relief for individuals experiencing frequent trips to the bathroom throughout the day or night.
remedy_properties: Isoflavones: Red clover tincture contains isoflavones, which have been shown to relax the urinary tract muscles and reduce symptoms of overactive bladder by increasing blood flow to the urethra and reducing inflammation in the bladder.

Phytoestrogens: Phytoestrogens in red clover tincture can help regulate hormonal imbalances that may contribute to overactive bladder, such as estrogen dominance or deficiency, which can relax or contract muscles in the urinary tract.

Antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds: Red clover tincture's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the bladder and urethra, which can alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder, such as frequency and urgency.
remedy_constituents: Isoflavones (specifically genistein and formononetin): These plant-derived estrogen compounds help to relax the bladder muscles and reduce symptoms of overactive bladder by mimicking the effects of estrogen in the body.

Phenolic acids (specifically kaempferol and quercetin): These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the bladder, contributing to the alleviation of overactive bladder symptoms.

Flavonoids (specifically apigenin): These compounds can help to relax the bladder muscles and improve bladder function by inhibiting the contraction of smooth muscle cells, leading to a reduction in symptoms of overactive bladder.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most used part due to their high content of isoflavones, which have been shown to have a positive effect on the bladder.
Roots: The roots are also used due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, which can help alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder.
Seeds: Red Clover seeds contain a high amount of isoflavones and other compounds that have been shown to have a beneficial effect on the bladder and urinary tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh red clover flowers and 1 part of fresh leaves in early summer morning.
Chop the harvested material into small pieces and combine with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks or until the liquid becomes dark brown.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids.
Store the trifolium pratense tincture in a cool dark place and use within 2 years or as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with overactive bladder because it contains a compound called podophyllotoxin, which has been shown to have a relaxing effect on the smooth muscle of the urinary tract. This can help to reduce the frequency and urgency of urination associated with an overactive bladder. Additionally, the tincture's antispasmodic properties can help to calm down contractions in the muscles of the bladder and urethra, providing relief from uncomfortable symptoms like incontinence and nocturia.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic: The Ginkgo biloba extract has been shown to relax smooth muscle tissue, reducing spasms and contractions in the bladder that can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory: Maidenhair Tree tincture contains flavonoids and terpenes that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the urinary tract and alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder such as urgency and frequency.

Uroprotective: Ginkgo biloba has been found to protect the bladder lining from damage and oxidative stress, potentially reducing the risk of bladder damage and inflammation that can contribute to overactive bladder.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the maidenhair tree contain compounds that have been shown to relax the muscles in the bladder and urinary tract, helping to alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes (underground stems) of the maidenhair tree are used to make tinctures that help reduce inflammation and irritation in the bladder and urinary tract, which can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of fresh or dried ginkgo biloba leaves from a trusted source and rinse thoroughly with distilled water.
Chop the ginkgo biloba leaves into small pieces and combine them with 250ml of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture at room temperature for 4 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction of active compounds.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and retaining 150ml of tincture.
Mix the remaining tincture with distilled water in a ratio of 1:2 (tincture to water) and fill dark glass dropper bottles for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sassafras tinctures helps with overactive bladder because of its unique ability to calm the muscles in the pelvic floor. The active compound, safrole, has been shown to reduce muscle spasms and contractions that can contribute to frequent urination and urgency. Additionally, sassafras has natural anti-inflammatory properties that may help soothe and protect the urinary tract, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with overactive bladder. By addressing these underlying issues, herbal sassafras tinctures can provide relief for individuals suffering from this common and often embarrassing condition.
remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Sassafras tincture's astringent properties help reduce inflammation and shrink swollen tissues in the urinary tract, which can alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder such as frequency and urgency.

Anti-inflammatory and anti-spasmodic properties: Sassafras tincture's anti-inflammatory and anti-spasmodic properties help soothe and relax the muscles in the pelvic floor, reducing muscle spasms that can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms like frequent urination and leakage.

Urinary tract antiseptic and anti-infective properties: Sassafras tincture's antibacterial and antifungal properties help prevent infections and reduce the growth of bacteria and fungi in the urinary tract, which can cause or exacerbate overactive bladder symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the sassafras plant contain a compound called safrole, which has been shown to have a relaxant effect on the muscles in the urinary tract, making it beneficial for reducing symptoms of overactive bladder.

Barks: The barks of the sassafras tree contain a compound called asafetida, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the urinary tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with overactive bladder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh sassafras albidum roots and leaves in the spring or early summer when the plant is at its peak potency.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator on low heat for 24 hours to preserve the bioactive compounds.
Chop the dried sassafras albidum roots and leaves into small pieces and combine with 60% vodka in a glass jar covering the plant material completely.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 14 days, shaking the jar daily, then strain the tincture through cheesecloth into another glass container discarding the solids.

Store the final sassafras albidum tincture in amber glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature for up to one year taking 10-15 drops three times a day as needed to relieve overactive bladder symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldthread tinctures helps with overactive bladder because it contains berberine, a natural compound that has been shown to relax the muscles in the urinary tract. This relaxation allows for smoother urine flow and reduced frequency of bathroom trips. Berberine also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe the bladder lining and reduce irritation caused by overactivity. Additionally, goldthread tinctures may help regulate bladder contractions, allowing individuals with overactive bladder to experience greater control and comfort throughout the day.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Goldthread tincture helps to relax the muscles in the urinary tract and reduce spasms, which can alleviate symptoms of an overactive bladder.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Goldthread has anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce inflammation and irritation in the urinary tract, which can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms.
Urethral toning properties: Goldthread tincture is believed to strengthen the urethral muscles, improving urethral sphincter function and reducing urgency and frequency of urination.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in berberine, a bioactive compound that has been shown to reduce bladder contractions and alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder.
Rhizomes: Containing a higher concentration of berberine than roots, rhizomes are often used to create more potent tinctures for overactive bladder relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried coptis chinensis roots in the spring or fall and clean them thoroughly.
Chop the cleaned roots into small pieces and combine 1 part root material with 2 parts 95% grain alcohol.
Transfer the mixture to a glass container and seal it tightly then store it in a cool dark place for 4 weeks.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids.
Bottle the filtered tincture in amber glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store it in the refrigerator at 39-40 degrees fahrenheit.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tinctures helps with overactive bladder because it possesses a unique combination of properties that address the underlying causes of this condition. The herb's ability to relax the muscles in the urinary tract helps to reduce urgency and frequency, while its anti-inflammatory effects soothe the mucous membranes and reduce irritation. Additionally, yarrow's antispasmodic properties help to calm bladder contractions, allowing for more efficient urination and reducing the likelihood of accidental leakage.
remedy_properties: Astringent and anti-inflammatory properties: Yarrow's astringent properties help reduce urine frequency by constricting the blood vessels in the urinary tract, while its anti-inflammatory properties alleviate irritation and inflammation that can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms.
Antispasmodic effects: Yarrow's antispasmodic properties help relax the muscles in the bladder and urethra, reducing muscle spasms and contractions that can lead to frequent or urgent urination.

Anti-inflammatory and soothing effects on the mucous membranes: Yarrow's anti-inflammatory and soothing properties help calm and protect the mucous membranes lining the urinary tract, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with overactive bladder symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Yarrow roots contain a high concentration of flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help soothe an overactive bladder.
Flowers: Yarrow flowers possess a unique combination of estrogenic and antispasmodic compounds that can help regulate the nervous system and reduce frequency and urgency of urination.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried achillea millefolium leaves stems and flowers at dawn when dew is present for maximum potency.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces then combine equal parts of leaf stem and flower for optimal extraction.
Mix 250 milliliters of highproof vodka with one cup of chopped plant material in a clean glass jar for maceration.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for four to six weeks allowing for maximum tincture extraction every two weeks.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container discarding the solids and labeling the final product.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock tinctures helps with overactive bladder because of its natural ability to soothe and calm the urinary tract. The anti-inflammatory properties of burdock root help to reduce irritation and inflammation in the bladder and urethra, which can contribute to frequent urination and urgency. Additionally, burdock's antioxidant properties help to protect the delicate tissues of the urinary tract from damage, promoting a healthy balance and reducing symptoms associated with overactive bladder.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Burdock tincture has anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce inflammation and irritation in the urinary tract, which can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms such as frequent urination.

Antispasmodic properties: The antispasmodic compounds in burdock tincture may help relax the muscles in the urinary tract and bladder wall, reducing spasms and cramping that can trigger sudden, intense urges to urinate.

Antioxidant properties: Burdock's antioxidant properties may help protect the bladder lining from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to bladder instability and overactive bladder symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in inulin, a prebiotic fiber that helps regulate bowel movements and alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder.
Fruits: Containing anthraquinone glycosides, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the urinary tract and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried arctium lappa roots at dawn or dusk for optimal potency and quality.
Chop the roots into small pieces and combine them with vodka in a clean glass jar at a ratio of 1:5.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool dark place for four weeks, shaking daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids after 30 minutes.
Store the tincture in dark glass bottles at room temperature for up to one year, using 20-40 drops as needed three times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm tinctures helps with overactive bladder because it contains mucilages, which soothe and protect the mucous membranes lining the urinary tract. This calming effect reduces irritation and inflammation, allowing for a smoother flow of urine and alleviating symptoms such as frequent urination, urgency, and incontinence. Additionally, slippery elm's anti-inflammatory properties help to relax the bladder muscles, further reducing symptoms and providing relief from overactive bladder episodes.
remedy_properties: Mucoadhesive properties: Slippery elm's mucilage helps soothe and protect the urinary tract lining, reducing inflammation and irritation that can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tannins present in slippery elm have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and discomfort in the bladder and urethra, alleviating overactive bladder symptoms such as frequent urination and urgency.

Soothing and calming effects: Slippery elm's mucilage has a calming effect on the urinary tract, helping to relax the muscles and reduce muscle spasms that can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms like incontinence and frequency.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ulmus rubra bark, clean it thoroughly with cold running water and dry it in a well-ventilated area for at least four days.
Grind the dried ulmus rubra bark into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle, approximately 10 grams per ounce of menstruum.
Combine the ground ulmus rubra bark with vodka as a menstruum in a clean glass jar, using a ratio of one part herb to three parts solvent, seal tightly and label.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for at least six weeks, shaking the jar twice daily to ensure proper extraction, allowing time for the active compounds to dissolve.

After six weeks, strain the tincture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another clean glass container, discard the solids and store the final product in a dark glass bottle with tight-fitting lid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder tinctures helps with overactive bladder because its bioflavonoids and terpenes have a calming effect on the urinary tract, reducing muscle spasms and frequency. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried sambucus nigra flowers and leaves in early summer when they are in full bloom.
Weigh 500 grams of the harvested material and chop it into small pieces to facilitate extraction.
Combine the chopped sambucus nigra with 1000 milliliters of vodka or neutral grain spirit in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for four weeks, shaking the jar daily to promote maceration.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth and discard the solids, then transfer the tincture to amber glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tinctures helps with overactive bladder because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the urinary tract and relax the bladder muscles. It has been traditionally used to calm irritations and spasms, reducing frequency and urgency. Additionally, ginger's antispasmodic effects help ease contractions, allowing the bladder to fill and empty properly. This can lead to a significant reduction in nighttime awakenings, frequency of trips to the bathroom, and overall improvement in urinary continence.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce inflammation in the bladder and urethra, which can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms like frequent urination and urgency.

Muscle relaxant properties: The bioactive compounds in ginger tincture, like gingerol and galangin, have a relaxant effect on smooth muscle tissue, including the muscles of the bladder and pelvic floor, helping to reduce spasms and discomfort associated with overactive bladder.

Antispasmodic properties: Ginger tincture's antispasmodic compounds help calm down uncontrolled muscle contractions in the bladder and urethra, reducing the frequency and intensity of urges to urinate and alleviating symptoms like incontinence.

Roots: Fresh ginger roots contain compounds like gingerol and shogaol that can help relax the muscles surrounding the urinary tract, alleviating symptoms of urgency and frequency.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger roots weighing at least one pound for optimal extract quality.
Chop the fresh ginger roots into small pieces and place them in a clean glass jar.
Combine the chopped ginger roots with 80 proof vodka filling the jar completely, allowing for minimal headspace.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for a minimum of six weeks to allow for steeping and infusion process.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids and store it in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with overactive bladder because it has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the urinary tract, reducing irritation and discomfort. The herb also has a natural ability to strengthen the muscles in the bladder wall, allowing for more efficient contractions and reducing the frequency of unwanted trips to the bathroom. Additionally, licorice root has been shown to help regulate hormones involved in bladder function, promoting a sense of calm and control over urinary habits.

Flavonoids: Possess anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation and irritation in the bladder lining, which can exacerbate overactive bladder symptoms.
Saponins: Act as a natural antispasmodic, calming down muscle contractions and relaxing the urethral sphincter to improve urinary control and reduce frequency and urgency.

Barks: Licorice barks contain flavonoids and triterpenoids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with overactive bladder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried root of glycyrrhiza glabra and store in an airtight container.
Measure out 250 grams of dried root and soak it in 750 milliliters of vodka for 14 days.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle discarding solids after pressing liquid gently.
Store the tincture at room temperature away from light for 6 months allowing flavors to mature gradually.
Decant and transfer the resulting tincture into smaller dark-colored bottles using clean equipment.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to overactive bladder that are treated with herbal tinctures are urinary frequency disorders such as urgency incontinence, nocturia, and frequency. Herbal tinctures like Uva Ursi (Arctostaphylos uva-ursi) and Corn Silk (Zea mays) have been traditionally used to soothe the bladder and reduce symptoms of these conditions. These herbal remedies may help alleviate discomfort, frequency, and urgency by relaxing the urinary tract muscles and reducing inflammation.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with overactive bladder is typically a blend of Urva Ursi, Corn Silk, and Dandelion Root. Urva Ursi helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms, while Corn Silk soothes and calms the urinary tract. Dandelion Root stimulates digestion and supports the elimination of excess fluids, which can contribute to overactive bladder. Taking 20-30 drops of each tincture in water, 2-3 times a day, can help to alleviate symptoms and promote overall urinary health.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Acta urologica Belgica, herbal tinctures, specifically Echinaceae and Sabalae extracts, may be beneficial in managing overactive bladder due to their ability to facilitate neurotransmission and activate smooth muscle fibers. In this study, 20 patients with idiopathic large hypotonic bladders were treated with a combination of Echinaceae (84-112 mg/day) and Sabalae (78-104 mg/day) extracts, which resulted in a significant decrease in bladder capacity, residual urine, and compliance, as well as an increase in detrusor pressure and peak flow. These findings suggest that herbal tinctures may offer a natural alternative for treating overactive bladder, potentially reducing symptoms and improving bladder function in affected individuals.
